title: ranunculus decorifactus
side_effects: Ranunculus decorifactus can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant's sap or leaves can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds like ranunculin and protoanemonin. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also lead to skin blisters, ulcers, and scarring. If ingested or improperly handled, ranunculus decorifactus can cause more severe symptoms, including respiratory failure and cardiac arrest in extreme cases.
